evidence.codeInspection: Mutation records exist but no utility to reconstruct tension state from mutation history
evidence.note: The validation contract describes this as a requirement, but the implementation does not include state reconstruction from mutation history

assertions.issues: Feature not implemented. The contract asserts that replaying mutations should reproduce current tension state, but no reconstruction utility exists.

frictions.description: VAL-MUTATION-007 (mutations_between) and VAL-MUTATION-011 (state reconstruction) reference functionality not present in the codebase. The validation contract specifies these as requirements but the Store module lacks: (1) a time-range query function mutations_between(start, end), and (2) a state reconstruction utility to replay mutation history.

blockers.description: Two validation assertions reference unimplemented functionality: (1) mutations_between time range query - not in Store API, (2) state reconstruction from mutation history - no replay utility exists. These are specification-to-implementation gaps, not test failures.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified codebase lacks these functions via grep and code inspection. Not a test failure - feature gap.

summary: Tested 11 assertions: 9 passed, 2 blocked (VAL-MUTATION-007, VAL-MUTATION-011). All implemented mutation functionality works correctly: creation/update/resolve all record proper mutations, failed operations leave no mutation trace, queries return ordered results, count is accurate, immutability enforced via private fields, all traits implemented. Blocked assertions reference features not yet implemented (time range queries and state reconstruction).
